subject: LU-529: Not mix caching and direct pages in RPC

messages.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/1495/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on rhel/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/4ad996be-b8af-11e0-8bdf-52540025f9af).  Ran 12 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1:

I'm not sure how did you define the direct io on obdfilter, and I don't see why do you think obdilter should use different way to submit the dio pages and buffered pages from client?

I agree with you that mixing dio page and cached page in same RPC is not good,  but I'd like a thorough way to fix it (don't mix them at the begining). If there isn't any outstanding bug caused by this, I don't think we need to land this temporary fix now.

Patch Set 1:

>I'm not sure how did you define the direct io on obdfilter, and I don't >see why do you think obdilter should use different way to submit >the dio pages and buffered pages from client?

Ideally we should do DirectIO on the obdfilter as well. Obdfilter used to do direct always, but this is not true after read cache is implemented. I just leave this flag for future use.

>I agree with you that mixing dio page and cached page in same RPC >is not good, but I'd like a thorough way to fix it (don't mix them at >the begining). If there isn't any outstanding bug caused by this, I >don't think we need to land this temporary fix now.

After we took TRANSIENT pages out of radix tree, it seems there is no way to prevent pages with the same offset from being added to one RPC, in case cache and direct IO is doing to the same file meanwhile.

Please let me know if I missed something.

Patch Set 1:

Hi, Xiong

I think that dio or buffered io is only meaningful for client, ost should know nothing about it, and no matter if the read cached is introduced, ost should always use the same way to transfer the data from client (dio pages or cached pages). Do you agree with me on this point?

Right, with current implementation, it's possible to have two pages (dio page and cached page) with same index in one RPC, but I just think it's not a fatal error, it's not necessary to introduce this patch to fix it.  Did I miss anything on this?

Patch Set 1:

"I think that dio or buffered io is only meaningful for client, ost should know nothing about it, and no matter if the read cached is introduced, ost should always use the same way to transfer the data from client (dio pages or cached pages). Do you agree with me on this point?"

Yes, I think this is quite reasonable. I meant to say "ideally" obdfilter should use DirectIO for DIO pages as well.

"Right, with current implementation, it's possible to have two pages (dio page and cached page) with same index in one RPC, but I just think it's not a fatal error, it's not necessary to introduce this patch to fix it. Did I miss anything on this?"

It looks like it will have two nio_buf(nio_count is 2) if there are two page with the same index in one RPC, it might be okay.

Patch Set 1:

>> I think that dio or buffered io is only meaningful for client, ost should know nothing about it, and no matter if the read cached
> Yes, I think this is quite reasonable. I meant to say "ideally" obdfilter should use DirectIO for DIO pages as well.

On my side, i think we should do direct i/o all the down to the disk, this includes server. I even made a patch for this some time ago for Cray:
https://bugzilla.lustre.org/attachment.cgi?id=29779

> Right, with current implementation, it's possible to have two pages (dio page and cached page) with same index in one RPC,

Is this because transient pages are no longer added to the radix tree?

Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(2 inline comments)

I'm fine with the patch, although most of this code is totally rewritten by the new RPC engine which processes transient and cached pages differently already.

Patch Set 1:

>On my side, i think we should do direct i/o all the down to the disk, this includes server. I even made a patch for this some time ago for Cray: https://bugzilla.lustre.org/attachment.cgi?id=29779

Hmm, I don't quite see why you want to purge cache on server for dio, to improve performance or some other purpose? Could you shed some light on this?

>Is this because transient pages are no longer added to the radix tree?

Yes, indeed.

Patch Set 1:

> Hmm, I don't quite see why you want to purge cache on server > for dio, to improve performance or some other purpose?
> Could you shed some light on this?

From the man page:
"O_DIRECT (Since Linux 2.4.10)
       Try  to  minimize  cache  effects of the I/O to and from this file.  In general this will degrade
       performance, but it is useful in special situations, such  as  when  applications  do  their  own
       caching."

From my point of view, DIO means that the application wants no caching anywhere.
However, not everyone agree on the semantic, so i think both approaches can be justified and are fine.

Patch Set 1: Abandoned

This change is not needed any more after LU-247 is landed

parents.subject: LU-394: LND failure casued by discontiguous KIOV

commit.subject: LU-529: Not mix caching and direct pages in RPC
commit.message:
LU-529: Not mix caching and direct pages in RPC

Several reasons to do this:
1. Ideally, direct pages should be performed using direct IO on
   obdfilter as well;
2. Direct pages are not in radix tree at client side, it has risk
   to put two pages(direct & caching) with the same index into
   one RPC.

In this patch, I made a fix by not mixing transient and caching pages
in one RPC. Also, a flag OBD_BRW_DIO is introduced to instruct obdfilter
to perform direct IO for this RPC.

Change-Id: I0a809d4ddd9dc186bd85b4e01dc7e863ff887fe4
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jay@whamcloud.com>
